## [0.9.0] - 2026-07-20

An **MCP release**. Vayu now exposes its engine to AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Zed) over the **Model Context Protocol**, so an agent can inspect your collections and runs, send requests, drive and compare load tests, and read live metrics by connecting to the already-running app. The bulk of the change is new TypeScript in the Electron main process (`/app`) — an MCP server plus a client-side request-composition pipeline — and a new **Settings → MCP** panel. The C++ engine is untouched.

### Added
- **MCP server, hosted in the app.** A Model Context Protocol server runs alongside Vayu at `http://127.0.0.1:9877/mcp` over Streamable HTTP, built on the official MCP SDK and proxying the engine's existing API. A **stdio** entry point (`vayu mcp`-style CLI) is also included for stdio-only clients and headless/CI use. Connect from Settings with a one-command snippet, e.g. `claude mcp add --transport http vayu http://127.0.0.1:9877/mcp`.
- **A full tool set, grouped by capability.** *Read* — engine health, collections, requests, environments, runs, run reports, config, live metrics, and run comparison. *Execute* — send a single request or smoke-test a whole collection. *Write* — create a saved request, update an environment, or change engine config. *Load* — start and stop load tests. Tools carry rich descriptions and read-only/destructive hints, and several return structured results.
- **Requests are composed exactly as the app sends them.** `{{variables}}` are resolved (environment > collection chain > globals), `inherit` auth is resolved up the collection chain, and collection + request pre/post scripts are composed — so an agent's request carries the right values, authentication (bearer, basic, API key, OAuth 2.0 with the engine's token cache), and test scripts, just like clicking **Send**.
- **Resources & prompts.** Vayu data is attachable as MCP resources (`vayu://runs`, `vayu://collections`, `vayu://environments`, `vayu://config`, and per-run reports), and ready-made prompts (`summarize_run`, `compare_runs`, `diagnose_errors`, `suggest_load_profile`) give agents a running start.
- **Settings → MCP.** A new settings panel: live connection status, one-command connect snippets, the target allowlist editor, load caps, per-tool (and per-category) on/off controls, a write-access toggle, and a server on/off switch. Settings persist across restarts.
- **Safe by default.** The server binds to loopback only with DNS-rebinding protection; the **target allowlist is empty by default (deny all)**; load runs enforce hard RPS/concurrency/duration caps and a confirmation gate; and data/config writes are off until you enable them. See `SECURITY.md` for the threat model.

[0.9.0]: https://github.com/athrvk/vayu/compare/v0.8.0...v0.9.0
